danger_signals.visual_form: Bullet streams with varying density and trajectory; difficulty settings escalate bullet count visually
danger_signals.meaning: Identifies threat intensity and required evasion complexity

danger_signals.visual_form: Possessed space fighters with distinct visual distortion or corruption appearance
danger_signals.meaning: Marks hostile entities as anomalous/possessed rather than standard military threats

danger_signals.visual_form: Three hit-point health system with visual degradation across hits
danger_signals.meaning: Communicates remaining resilience; persistent health between levels raises stakes

danger_signals.visual_form: Walls and obstacles that require navigation via speed-switching mechanic
danger_signals.meaning: Indicates collision danger requiring active player response

safety_signals.visual_form: Dropped by defeated enemies; distinct collectible sprite
safety_signals.meaning: Offers healing (restores 1 HP) and weapon system upgrades simultaneously

safety_signals.visual_form: Player-controlled speed states (fast/medium/slow) affecting visual movement rate
safety_signals.meaning: Enables reactive safety through evasion or defensive positioning

safety_signals.visual_form: Weapon sacrifice triggers weapon-specific invulnerability or area-denial effects
Up to three stored weapons (L, H, E, R, A, B, C) with distinct visual firing patterns
safety_signals.meaning: Multiple tactical approaches reduce forced vulnerability to single threat type

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Orbiting security force base; research stations on planet Delia

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Possessed fighter visual corruption; unknown origin bioship signature
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Possessed planetary fighters (colonist technology) vs. organic alien bioships
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Emanate from Delia's atmosphere; hazardous breach origin

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Possessed fighters appear as corrupted versions of standard colonial defense craft
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Research stations and colonized areas as stage environments

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Delia colony and orbiting security force infrastructure intact
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Standard patrol patterns; organized defense response available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Possessed fighters with anomalous behavior; emergency conditions broadcast
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Transformed allied fighters become hostile; alien bioships operate in coordinated assault patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy bullet density increases proportionally; screen density reflects threat level
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Higher difficulty ('Funny' mode) dramatically escalates enemy firing rate and pattern complexity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Health indicator (3 HP maximum) degrades visually with each hit
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Persistent health between levels means damaged ship carries vulnerability forward; creates mounting tension

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Current equipped weapons display distinct firing patterns (laser beams, homing projectiles, spiraling attacks, etc.)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Smart Bomb sacrifice transitions weapon slots; strategic choice between offense and inventory space

environmental_storytelling.example: Player investigates disaster on Delia (2279 setting); environments transition from space to colony to alien-affected zones, visualizing scope of invasion
Possessed fighters vs. native bioships visually distinguish between compromised allied forces and true invaders, telling story of infiltration

environmental_storytelling.technique: Persistent health mechanic as narrative anchor
environmental_storytelling.example: Ship carries damage between levels, communicating 'Operation Climax' as continuous dangerous mission rather than discrete challenges
Enemies drop power-ups selectively; forces player to adapt tactics to available loadouts, reflecting improvisation under crisis conditions

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Responsive to speed-switching input; maintains directional control; fires selected weapon continuously
Health depletes by one point; ship remains functional but vulnerability increases; visual feedback signals damage received
Weapon is sacrificed; weapon-specific effect triggers (laser spin, homing field, antimatter reaction, melee drone, etc.); invulnerability window active (except for Ricochet drone variant)
Fire projectiles in escalating patterns; patrol and intercept; visual corruption distinguishes from uninfected craft
Coordinate attacks; possess unique firing patterns; boss-level variants demonstrate coordinated tactical organization
Remains stationary until collected; provides dual benefit (heal + upgrade); incentivizes enemy engagement

notes: Phalanx communicates world-state primarily through enemy behavior escalation and visual density rather than explicit UI narrative. The persistent health system is a core manifestation mechanic—damage carries forward between levels, creating a tension arc that accumulates danger. The faction conflict (security force vs. bioship invasion) is communicated through possessed fighter visual corruption and environmental context (colony under attack). The game uses speed-switching and weapon selection as player-agency signals: slow speed for precision safety, fast speed for aggressive evasion; weapon choice reflects tactical stance. Difficulty modes (standard vs. 'Funny' mode) are visible through bullet density and firing rate, creating graduated threat manifestation. The setting (year 2279, planet Delia, research colony) provides narrative context but minimal on-screen political situation detail; focus remains on immediate tactical survival.
